original_abstract:
This volume brings together leading international scholars to examine the profound structural and conceptual shifts currently reshaping higher education across the globe. As universities navigate an era of unprecedented change, the contributors investigate the complex interplay between traditional academic values and modern pressures, including the transition from mass education to market-driven systems. Through a diverse range of regional case studies—spanning Europe, Southeast Europe, East Asia, Mexico, and North America—the book analyzes how globalization and shifting welfare state dynamics are redefining the university’s mission.

Key themes explored include the persistent tension between global connectivity and local identity, the rise of "New Managerialism" and its impact on academic freedom, and the growing influence of privatization. Furthermore, the collection addresses the evolving student demographic, examining how "massification" impacts diversity and whether the long-heralded shift toward lifelong learning is finally becoming a reality. From the role of MOOCs and digital "unbundling" to the development of age-friendly institutions, this work provides a comprehensive overview of the reforms, challenges, and likely futures of higher education in the 21st century.
humanized_abstract:
This volume brings together leading international scholars to examine the profound structural and conceptual shifts currently reshaping higher education across the globe. As universities navigate an era of unprecedented change, the contributors investigate the complex interplay between traditional academic values and modern pressures, including the transition from mass education to market-driven systems. Through a diverse range of regional case studies—spanning Europe, Southeast Europe, East Asia, Mexico, and North America—the book analyzes how globalization and shifting welfare state dynamics are redefining the university’s mission.

This volume explores the key issues arising from these changes: • The tensions between globalisation and the localisation of higher education, particularly when it comes to issues of identity, language and devolution of power • The impact of the 'New Managerialism' and the ways in which this has affected academic freedom • The growing impact of private interests and markets on education The impact of 'massification' on diversity, and whether the promise of a learning society is still one that can be delivered to all. The role of MOOCs, and the effects of un-bundling and different business models on teaching and learning, as well as the ways in which HE institutions can become more age-friendly environments for students of all ages.
